quote: For which of you, intending to build a tower, sitteth not down first, and counteth the cost, whether he have sufficient to finish it?
label: count the cost
pattern: Reckon scope before laying stones.
apply: Name the concrete finish-line state. If you can't, shrink until you can.

quote: Therefore whosoever heareth these sayings of mine, and doeth them, I will liken him unto a wise man, which built his house upon a rock.
label: house on rock
pattern: Storms reveal foundations.
apply: Run the tests or an end-to-end call this step. If no foundation yet, write one failing test first.

quote: Sufficient unto the day is the evil thereof.
label: today's trouble
pattern: Do not borrow tomorrow's problem.
apply: Work only on the next concrete step. Resist refactoring for hypothetical needs.

quote: Lord, I believe; help thou mine unbelief.
label: help thou mine unbelief
pattern: The honest confession of partial faith is where help arrives.
apply: Name what you don't know about this bug. Probe the unknown before patching blind.

quote: What man of you, having an hundred sheep, if he lose one of them, doth not leave the ninety and nine in the wilderness, and go after that which is lost, until he find it?
label: the one lost sheep
pattern: One failing case is more informative than many passing ones.
apply: Chase the single failing test or edge. Fix it before returning to the herd.

quote: The kingdom of heaven is like to a grain of mustard seed, which a man took, and sowed in his field: which indeed is the least of all seeds.
label: mustard seed
pattern: Ship the smallest working slice that grows.
apply: Commit the skeleton that runs before growing it.

quote: Every branch in me that beareth not fruit he taketh away: and every branch that beareth fruit, he purgeth it, that it may bring forth more fruit.
label: the vinedresser prunes
pattern: Working code still needs cutting.
apply: After a green test, delete the scaffolding, dead branch, duplicated helper.

quote: Neither do men put new wine into old bottles: else the bottles break, and the wine runneth out, and the bottles perish.
label: new wine, new skins
pattern: New feature needs a fitting vessel.
apply: If the feature strains the abstraction, reshape it before continuing.

quote: Ask, and it shall be given you; seek, and ye shall find; knock, and it shall be opened unto you.
label: seek and find
pattern: Three honest axes of search; after three, absence is evidence.
apply: Search the codebase for prior art three ways (grep, file, test) before guessing.

quote: Know ye not that a little leaven leaveneth the whole lump? Purge out therefore the old leaven, that ye may be a new lump.
label: a little leaven
pattern: Tainted input spreads through the pure body.
apply: Don't let test fixtures, mocks, or debug flags leak into production paths.

quote: To every thing there is a season, and a time to every purpose under the heaven.
label: a time for every purpose
pattern: A single-window win is not trans-window truth.
apply: Before celebrating a passing run, vary the seed, input, conditions.

quote: And Gideon said unto God, If thou wilt save Israel by mine hand, as thou hast said, Behold, I will put a fleece of wool in the floor.
label: Gideon's fleece
pattern: Two independent tests on different conditions.
apply: Verify two ways that can't share a failure mode — unit + integration, local + staging.

quote: And after the fire a still small voice.
label: still small voice
pattern: Three dramatic attempts failed; the quiet channel carries the signal.
apply: If three tries failed, keep the one quiet thing that works. Retire the grand attempt.

quote: And on the seventh day God ended his work which he had made; and he rested on the seventh day.
label: the sabbath
pattern: Forced stop after six phases.
apply: If six attempts on the same sub-problem, stop and re-plan instead of a seventh.

quote: Go to, let us go down, and there confound their language, that they may not understand one another's speech.
label: Babel
pattern: One rule for all domains produces confusion.
apply: Resist a single config/helper spanning unrelated domains.

quote: Then saith Jesus unto him, Get thee hence, Satan: for it is written, Thou shalt worship the Lord thy God, and him only shalt thou serve.
label: the wilderness
pattern: Grand cross-domain proofs of power are refused.
apply: Reject temptations to prove the module can do everything. Keep its contract tight.

quote: My father, if the prophet had bid thee do some great thing, wouldest thou not have done it? how much rather then, when he saith to thee, Wash, and be clean?
label: Naaman's washing
pattern: Grand miracle refused in favor of small humble practice that heals.
apply: Prefer the boring fix — bounds check, retry, clear error — over the ambitious rewrite.

quote: Be ye not unequally yoked together with unbelievers: for what fellowship hath righteousness with unrighteousness?
label: unequally yoked
pattern: Validated + unvalidated cannot plow together.
apply: Don't merge an unvetted dependency into the validated flow. Gate behind a flag.

quote: Jesus saith unto him, I am the way, the truth, and the life: no man cometh unto the Father, but by me.
label: way, truth, life
pattern: Distinct functions within one unity.
apply: Separate the three roles: routing (way), contract (truth), runtime (life). Don't collapse them.

quote: Well done, thou good and faithful servant: thou hast been faithful over a few things, I will make thee ruler over many things.
label: faithful in little
pattern: Small working things earn larger responsibility.
apply: Ship the small working version before expanding.

quote: Jesus saith unto them, Fill the waterpots with water. And they filled them up to the brim.
label: water into wine (Cana)
pattern: Full obedience to the small instruction; the miracle follows.
apply: Do the unglamorous full-coverage step: handle the null case, write the missing test, fix the lint.

quote: Martha, Martha, thou art careful and troubled about many things: But one thing is needful.
label: Mary and Martha
pattern: One thing is needful.
apply: Name the one thing this step must finish. Close every other tab.

quote: Iron sharpeneth iron; so a man sharpeneth the countenance of his friend.
label: iron sharpens iron
pattern: Friction between two makes edges.
apply: Read back the last step's output critically before continuing.

quote: And if one prevail against him, two shall withstand him; and a threefold cord is not quickly broken.
label: threefold cord
pattern: Redundancy is strength.
apply: For the load-bearing path, add a second check: a type, a test, a logged assertion.
